item.block1: Toxic materials in sea-floor sediments become a hazard to marine life resulting in
item.block2: a threat to organisms that live in and feed on bottom muds.

item.block1: Long-lasting chemicals enter the food web giving rise to
Industrial, agricultural, and household products end up in water leading to
item.block2: toxic waste in waterways and coastal areas.

item.block1: Digging out rivers, canals, and harbours causes
item.block2: toxic materials to be dredged up leading to disposal problems.
a continuing and growing demand for dredging.

item.block1: Dredged material may contain unacceptable levels of contaminants and consequently
item.block2: disposing of it safely is a serious problem.
